request.question: Baby stars which American actor born on October 20, 1971?

title: Robert Lesser
Baby (2007 film)
Kenneth Choi
Henry James Montague
Robert Peters
Ethlyne Clair
Eli Nadel
Michael Romanoff
Mary Carlisle
Sasha Neulinger
sentences: Robert Lesser or Bobby Lesser (born October 22, 1942) is an American actor born in New York City and now living in Santa Barbara, California.
His earliest work dates back to 1967 with "David Holzman's Diary", directed by Jim McBride.
His latest movies include the Japanese film "Best Wishes for Tomorrow" and post-production "Painting in the Rain".
Lesser has worked with Bruce Willis, Ed Harris, Alan Arkin, Arnold Schwarzenegger, Matthew Broderick, Dennis Quaid, Penelope Ann Miller, and Richard Mulligan.
He is well known in the Santa Barbara theater community.
Baby is a 2007 independent film, considered part of the hood film genre.
The film tells the story of an Asian-American youth's gang life in East Los Angeles, set during the mid '80s to the early '90s.
Directed by Juwan Chung and starring David Huynh, Tzi Ma, Feodor Chin, Ron Yuan and Kenneth Choi.
It has been called "the Asian American Boyz n the Hood" by the San Francisco Chronicle.

The film won Best Narrative Feature at the 2007 Los Angeles Asian Pacific Film Festival and a Special Jury Award for "Outstanding Newcomer" for star David Huynh at the same film festival that year, and a Best Director award for Director Juwan Chung at the 2008 DisOrient Film Festival.
It was also distributed by Lionsgate.
Kenneth Choi (born October 20, 1971) is an American actor.
He is best known for playing the character Henry Lin on the television series "Sons of Anarchy" (2008–2014).
He is also known for his roles as Jim Morita in the film "" (2011), Chester Ming in Martin Scorsese's "The Wolf of Wall Street" (2013), Judge Lance Ito in "" (2016).
and Lewis on the FOX comedy series "The Last Man on Earth".
Henry James Montague was the stage name of Henry John Mann, (January 20, 1843 – August 13, 1878), an American actor born in England.
Robert Louis Peters (October 20, 1924 – June 13, 2014) was an American poet, critic, scholar, playwright, editor, and actor born in an impoverished rural area of northern Wisconsin in 1924.
He held a Ph.D in Victorian literature.
His poetry career began in 1967 when his young son Richard died unexpectedly of spinal meningitis.
The book commemorating this loss, "Songs for a Son", was selected by poet Denise Levertov to be published by W. W. Norton in 1967, and it still remains in print.
"Songs for a Son" began a flood of poetry.
Born Ethlyne Williamson, Ethlyne Clair (23 November 1904 – 27 February 1996) was an American actress.
She was a born in Talladega, Alabama.
A former New York art student, she appeared mostly in silent film, including several Westerns where she played the love interest to Hoot Gibson.
She was succeeded by Derelys Perdue in the "Newlyweds" film series, in which she appeared in 1927 and 1928.
She appeared in the serials "The Vanishing Rider" (1928) and "Queen of the Northwoods" (1929).
She was a WAMPAS Baby Stars alumna.
Eliah Maurice Nadel (October 9, 1918 – March 9, 1981) was an American actor born in the Bronx, New York.
Michael Romanoff, pseudonym for Harry F. Gerguson, born Hershel Geguzin, (February 20, 1890 – September 1, 1971) was a Hollywood restaurateur, conman, and actor born in Lithuania.
He is perhaps best known as the owner of the now-defunct Romanoff's, a Beverly Hills restaurant popular with Hollywood stars in the 1940s and 1950s.
Mary Carlisle (born February 3, 1912 or 1914; sources differ) is a retired American actress, singer, and dancer.
Raised in Boston, Massachusetts, she starred in several Hollywood films in the 1930s, having been one of 15 girls selected as WAMPAS Baby Stars in 1932.
Her first major role was in the 1933 film "College Humor" with Bing Crosby.
The two went on to perform together in two additional films, "Double or Nothing" (1937) and "Doctor Rhythm" (1938).
Carlisle retired from her acting career shortly after her marriage in 1942, with "Dead Men Walk" (1943) being her final film credit.
Sasha Joseph Neulinger is an American actor born on September 20, 1989, in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Task: Compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a single concise, retrieval-oriented intermediate state to guide the next-hop retrieval. Do not produce the final answer or perform any new retrieval here.

Required content of `summary` (single field):
- A short header identifying the primary topic(s) found in the passages (1 line).
- A concise enumeration of the concrete facts supported verbatim by the provided passages: list entity mentions and directly supported factual spans (dates, years, titles, roles). Use very short quoted fragments (<= ~15 words) only when those words appear verbatim in the passages. Keep to facts present in the passages; do not add or infer new facts.
- Explicitly expose any ambiguity or missing information that blocks answering: if multiple candidate entities or page variants appear, enumerate those candidates and label the name as ambiguous; if a required target fact is missing or ambiguous, label it as the missing fact and identify the bridge entity that connects this hop to the next.
- Prioritized next-hop retrieval directives (1–4 items): concrete page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, explicit article sections to inspect (e.g., lede/infobox, "Career", "Publication", "History"), or tight search keywords. Order them by priority and be specific (include possible disambiguation terms or section names).

Formatting and style constraints:
- Keep the entire `summary` concise: a short header plus 1–4 brief sentences or 2–6 short bullet lines in total.
- Preserve the habit of extracting and citing concrete facts verbatim from the passages (names, dates, passage labels).
- Do not output a final answer, comparison, or any numeric fact that purports to resolve the question here. Even if the passages fully and unambiguously contain all required facts, present that evidence and explicitly state "no additional retrieval needed," but defer producing any final answer to a downstream component.
- If a name or term has multiple distinct variants in the passages, do not pick one without disambiguating evidence; enumerate candidates instead.
- Do not invent or add facts not present in the supplied passages. Avoid long verbatim quotations; prefer short extracts (<= ~15 words) or paraphrase.

Output expectations: produce exactly one field `summary` containing the concise, retrieval-oriented summary that meets the above requirements.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, assert hidden facts, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions (must follow exactly):
- Emit a single field named `summary` containing only 1–3 concise sentences that integrate the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into a grounded post-retrieval state.
- If the `context` already contains a concise answer sentence, keep that sentence as the first sentence of `summary` unless the `passages` clearly contradict it; append supporting sentence(s) after it rather than replacing it.
- Preserve the primary answer name(s) exactly as they appear in the inputs (context or passages) unless those names clearly conflict with stronger passage content.
- When one or more `passages` supply short biographical, membership, partnership, or corroborating facts about a named entity from the `context`, append at most one brief supporting sentence per relevant named person. Each such supporting sentence should:
  - begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the passages (if available),
  - paraphrase up to one or two key attributes present in the passages (examples: nationality, award/winner status, current affiliation/team, partner identity, founding/formation fact, birth date), and
  - avoid long verbatim copying (prefer paraphrase; use short quotes only when a brief exact phrase from a passage is necessary for clarity).
- If the question requires naming both members of a partnership or collaborator pair, name both people in the summary and, if passages provide partner-specific attributes, include one brief attribute for each as separate short clauses or sentences if needed.
- Do not invent facts not present in the provided passages. If passages conflict on a specific attribute, do not synthesize a new reconciled fact; instead prefer the passage that most directly addresses the question or omit the disputed attribute (or state necessary ambiguity only when needed to avoid a false claim).
- Keep the `summary` compact and focused: avoid extraneous biography, discography/chart data, or unrelated commentary. When `context` is absent or is not a concise answer, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary naming the relevant entity(ies) and including at most one brief paraphrased attribute drawn from the passages.

Output requirement: exactly one JSON field `summary` whose value is the integrated 1–3 sentence summary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only `summary_1` and `summary_2` as the evidential basis for the final decision; do not add new facts or external information.
- First determine whether the question is a binary (yes/no) proposition and whether both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary outcome.
- If BOTH summaries unambiguously assert the same binary conclusion, emit exactly one lowercase token as the entire content of `answer`: use "yes" for an affirmative conclusion and "no" for a negative conclusion. The `answer` field must contain that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no extra whitespace, no explanation).
- Prefer `summary_2` as the decisive statement when it clarifies or restates `summary_1`, but still require that both summaries support the same binary judgment before emitting the single-token `answer`.
- If the summaries conflict, if either summary hedges, is ambiguous, silent about the binary relation, or otherwise fails to clearly agree, do NOT apply the single-token rule; instead produce the module's normal fallback/unresolved output for `answer`.
- Always remain truthful to the visible summaries and do not alter their factual conclusions.

response.hop1_query: Baby stars which American actor born on October 20, 1971?
hop1_docs: Eric Scott (actor) | Eric Scott (born Eric Scott Magat; October 20, 1958, in Los Angeles, California) is an American actor whose most noted role is as Ben Walton, which he first played in the movie "" in 1971, and subsequently in the series "The Waltons".

Chris Sligh | Charles Christopher Sligh (born July 20, 1978) is an American singer; songwriter and producer who was a finalist on the sixth season of "American Idol", finishing in tenth place.  He also starred in "October Baby".

Feodor Chin | Feodor Chin (born August 18, 1974) is an American actor, writer, voiceover artist, producer and director.  Chin starred as the antagonist "Benny" in Juwan Chung's "Baby" opposite David Huynh, the protagonist.  "Baby" won a Special Jury Award for Best Feature Length Film, Narrative at the 2007 Los Angeles Asian Pacific Film Festival, among other awards.  He also starred as pioneering actor and artist Keye Luke in Timothy Tau's short film bio-pic "Keye Luke", which premiered at the 2012 Los Angeles Asian Pacific Film Festival and which was also Closing Night Film of the inaugural 2013 Seattle Asian American Film Festival.  Chin also voices the character of Zenyatta from Blizzard Entertainment's game, "Overwatch".

Diana Serra Cary | Diana Serra Cary (born Peggy-Jean Montgomery, October 29, 1918), known as Baby Peggy, is an American former child actress, author and historian.  Although other child actors from the time are still living, she is the last living film star of the silent era.
Matthew Godfrey (actor) | Matthew Godfrey (born October 20, 1971 in Ottawa, Ontario) is a Canadian actor and voice actor.  He is a graduate of the National Theatre School of Canada.

Rajesh Kumar (actor) | Rajesh Kumar (born 20 January 1976) is an Indian actor, best known for his role as Subodh Thakkar in the Star Plus hit comedy-drama series "Baa Bahoo Aur Baby" and as Rosesh Sarabhai in Star One hit sitcom Sarabhai vs Sarabhai and the sequel . He is from Patna, Bihar and is an alumunus of Hindu College, University of Delhi.

Chad Willett | Chad Willett (born October 10, 1971, British Columbia) is a Canadian actor and producer who has worked for over 20 years as a professional in film, television and theatre.  His films include "Hector and the Search for Happiness", starring Simon Pegg, and "Monster Trucks" directed by Chris Wedge.
response.summary_1:
Primary topics: the film "Baby" (and similarly titled "October Baby") and actors' birthdates/nationalities.

- "Feodor Chin (born August 18, 1974) is an American actor" and "Chin starred as the antagonist 'Benny' in Juwan Chung's 'Baby'".
- "Matthew Godfrey (born October 20, 1971 in Ottawa, Ontario) is a Canadian actor" — shows an Oct 20, 1971 birth but Canadian, not American.
- "Chris Sligh ... also starred in 'October Baby'." — indicates a different film title ("October Baby") appears in the passages.
- "Eric Scott (born ... October 20, 1958) is an American actor" and "Chad Willett (born October 10, 1971) is a Canadian actor" — other Oct birthdates present but not Oct 20, 1971 American.

Ambiguities / missing facts:
- Ambiguous film title: passages reference both "Baby" (Juwan Chung's film) and "October Baby" (different film). Label: film-title ambiguous.
- Missing fact: no passage identifies "an American actor born October 20, 1971" who starred in "Baby". Bridge entity needed: the specific "Baby" film's cast list (which actor with birthdate Oct 20, 1971, and their nationality).

Prioritized next-hop retrievals:
1. Fetch "Baby (2007 film)" — inspect lede/infobox and "Cast" section for actor names and birthdates/nationalities.
2. Fetch "October Baby" (film) — inspect lede/infobox and "Cast" section to rule out confusion with "Baby".
3. Search/Fetch "List of people born October 20, 1971" or run query: site:wikipedia.org "born October 20, 1971" "actor" to find American actors with that birthdate.
4. If Matthew Godfrey appears as a candidate, fetch "Matthew Godfrey (actor)" lede/infobox to confirm nationality and film credits.
response.hop2_query: Baby film cast actor born October 20 1971 nationality
hop2_docs: Matthew Godfrey (actor) | Matthew Godfrey (born October 20, 1971 in Ottawa, Ontario) is a Canadian actor and voice actor.  He is a graduate of the National Theatre School of Canada.
Julika Jenkins | Julika Jenkins (born 20 October 1971) is a German actress.  She appeared in more than twenty films since 1994.  She lives in Berlin with her partner, actor Arnd Klawitter.

George Harris (actor) | George William Harris (born 20 October 1949) is a British actor of film, stage, television, radio and musical theatre.  His notable roles include Kingsley Shacklebolt in the "Harry Potter" film series, Captain Simon Katanga in "Raiders of the Lost Ark" and Clive King in popular medical drama "Casualty", where he was one of the original cast members.  He also played real-life Somali warlord Osman Ali Atto in the 2001 film "Black Hawk Down".

Red Sun | Red Sun (French: "Soleil rouge") is a 1971 French-Italian-Spanish Western film with an international cast.  It stars American-born actor Charles Bronson, Japanese actor Toshirō Mifune, French actor Alain Delon and Swiss actress Ursula Andress.  It was filmed in Spain by the British director Terence Young.  It was released in the United States in 1972.

George Nichols (actor) | George Nichols (October, 1864 – September 20, 1927) was an American actor and film director.  He appeared in 221 films between 1908 and 1928.  He also directed 103 films between 1911 and 1916.  He was born in Rockford, Illinois and died in Hollywood, California.  He, along with Henry "Pathe" Lehrman, became an arch-enemy of Charlie Chaplin, who would take direction from nobody while at Keystone.  'Pops' as he was known, was often cast as father to Mabel Normand.

Chad Willett | Chad Willett (born October 10, 1971, British Columbia) is a Canadian actor and producer who has worked for over 20 years as a professional in film, television and theatre.  His films include "Hector and the Search for Happiness", starring Simon Pegg, and "Monster Trucks" directed by Chris Wedge.

Simhada Mari Sainya | Simhada Mari Sainya (Kannada:ಸಿಂಹದ ಮರಿ ಸೈನ್ಯ) is a 1981 Indian Kannada language children's film directed by Rajendra Singh Babu.  It stars Arjun Sarja (credited as Master Arjun Sarja), Amrish Puri and Rekha (credited as Baby Rekha) in the lead roles.  The supporting cast features an ensemble of other child actors.  It won two awards at the 1981–82 Karnataka State Film Awards — Best Editor (K. Balu) and Best Female Child Actor (Baby Rekha).
response.summary_2: None of the provided passages identify an American actor born October 20, 1971 as starring in Baby. Matthew Godfrey was born October 20, 1971 but is described in the passages as a Canadian actor (born in Ottawa, Ontario).
response.answer: None of the provided passages identify an American actor born October 20, 1971 who starred in Baby. Matthew Godfrey is listed as born October 20, 1971 but is described in the passages as Canadian.
